definition: Software whose source code is publicly available for anyone to view, modify, and distribute.
extendedDefinition: Open-source software is developed collaboratively by communities of developers who contribute code, fix bugs, and review each other's work. Licensing models like MIT, GPL, and Apache define how the software can be used, modified, and redistributed. Many modern SaaS platforms are built on open-source foundations such as Linux, PostgreSQL, and React. The open-source model promotes transparency, security through collective auditing, and rapid innovation.
description: Open-source software has source code that anyone can inspect, modify, and enhance. Learn how open-source tools drive innovation in the software industry.
